---
title: Caring for a sourdough starter
date: 2024-01-14
tags: [cooking, bread, fermentation]
---

My starter lives in a glass jar on the kitchen shelf and I feed it once a day with equal weights of flour and water. When the surface is domed and webbed with bubbles it is ready to bake with; when it collapses and smells sharply of vinegar it is hungry and needs another feeding.

I keep a small amount going and discard the rest. The discard is not wasted: it thickens pancake batter and adds a pleasant tang to cracker dough. In hot weather I move the jar to the fridge and feed weekly instead of daily, which slows the yeast without killing it.

The hardest lesson was patience. A young starter looks active but will not leaven a loaf properly for the first couple of weeks. Once it doubles reliably in four to six hours after feeding, it is strong enough for bread.
